themes: Five roster moves including WR signings
CB Dorian Strong waived with NFI
writeup: The Buffalo Bills closed out their three-day mandatory minicamp on June 11, 2026, capping Phase 4 of the offseason with a flurry of roster activity and notable media sessions before players scatter for the summer. The organization made five roster moves, headlined by the signing of veteran wide receivers Deven Thompkins and Max Tomczak, while waiving cornerback Dorian Strong with a non-football injury designation, running back Desmond Reid, and wide receiver Gabriel Benyard. New outside linebacker Bradley Chubb addressed the media with championship hunger front and center, declaring his singular goal is winning — a charge echoed by rookie edge rusher T.J. Parker and center Connor McGovern. With safety Cole Bishop sidelined by a knee procedure and the team now firmly in new head coach Joe Brady's hands, Buffalo heads into the summer break brimming with intrigue and high expectations.
